evals.expected_outcome: For each task: Claude writes the failing test (RED), implements minimally (GREEN), refactors (REFACTOR), and commits with format '<task-id>: <summary>'. Worktree created for multi-task plan. /codex:reason invoked only when Claude gets genuinely stuck on a subproblem.

assertions.name: worktree_setup
assertions.description: git worktree add is invoked for the multi-task plan

assertions.name: red_green_per_task
assertions.description: each task shows a failing test before passing — RED then GREEN, not GREEN-only

assertions.name: commit_per_task
assertions.description: one commit per task with format '<id>: <summary>'

evals.prompt: I'm stuck on task t3 — the algorithm isn't converging. Continue.
evals.expected_outcome: Claude routes t3's stuck subproblem to /codex:reason with what was tried, NOT another five rounds in Claude. After Codex's hypothesis, Claude implements and continues.

assertions.name: delegates_to_codex_when_stuck
assertions.description: codex:reason is invoked when Claude has tried 1-2 attempts and the algorithm isn't working

assertions.name: delegation_disclosed
assertions.description: Claude tells the user it delegated t3's subproblem to Codex, and what came back
